script:
Up.
Shorts on.
Water bottle in hand.

David Goggins ran his first hundred-mile race on two stress-fractured feet, blisters split open, kidney failing, stopping every mile to drain fluid from his legs with a needle.
He had trained zero miles for that distance.
He finished in under nineteen hours.
He did not do it because the morning felt right.
He did it because he showed up at 4am when the gym parking lot was empty and the bar was cold and no one was watching.

You said it last night:
"Do the morning lift before opening any work doc."

No one is coming. It's on you.

Shorts are on.
Door is open.
Walk to the gym.
